page: Microsoft Azure FX48-24ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 12 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores running at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions). This instance is equipped with 1008.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory and single-core performance. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core testing and text processing. Large-block memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 187.78 GB/sec for read-only operations and 120.35 GB/sec for write operations. Single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench image processing also rank in the top 25%. For AI workloads, the system demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds of 336.04 tokens/sec using a small 135M LLM model, while maintaining average performance on larger models and multi-core operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX48-24ms_v2 offers a highly specialized hardware profile that trades local storage and GPU capabilities for massive memory density and high-frequency CPU cores. This makes it a qualitatively cost-effective choice for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe drives but benefit from high-speed RAM and strong single-thread execution. It is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy text processing, enterprise applications, and memory-bound analytical workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure FX48-24ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server from the FXmsv2 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 24 dedicated vCPUs across 12 physical cores with a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz and a 260 MB L3 cache. The server is configured with 1008.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core execution, text processing, and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications that require high-frequency processing without local storage. It is best suited for in-memory databases, complex text processing, and memory-bound enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX48-24ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 vCPUs, 1008 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier single-core and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive server featuring massive RAM capacity and advanced Intel processor technologies for demanding memory-bound workloads.
